sources: Avoma Discovery Call, 17 Dec 2025 (key_takeaways: '7 days to 9 months')
metrics.narrative: Pain is quantified in operational terms but not in dollars or ROI. Christina Behm told Zycus on 21 Nov 2025 that supplier onboarding turnaround 'really takes less than 7 days to 9 months,' and the 17 Dec 2025 discovery call logged the same manual-onboarding time variance as a key pain point. No budget figure, cost-of-inaction, or ROI target has been shared in any call or field to date.

champion.narrative: Christina Behm has functioned as the consistent internal driver — coordinating every demo, requesting quotes, and relaying status — across the full 21 Nov 2025 to 29 Jun 2026 history. However, she has not demonstrated access to power: her most recent updates (26 May and 9 Jun 2026) cite an opaque 'internal approvals' process she does not control, and she has not surfaced Tim Drost or any executive back into the conversation since Dec 2025.

competition.narrative: The only alternative on record is Pep's own IT team extending its internal 'Pep Connect' platform. Tim Drost stated on 17 Dec 2025: 'we looked at building this, and it still could happen... we truly are engaging a number of folks and gonna make a decision here on a path forward.' No external vendor has ever been named in any call, Next Step, or Salesforce competitor field.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is well-documented and consistent across calls. On 21 Nov 2025, Christina Behm described undocumented liability/insurance exceptions living only in staff memory; on 17 Dec 2025, Tim Drost named 'the insight and visibility into what the agreements might say into the day to day' as 'probably one of our biggest pain points,' and Jillian Lushman confirmed 'you just don't have a way to document any exceptions or nuances, and that's been a struggle when it's just living in our heads.'

sources: Next_Step_History__c (full dated trail, no paper-process mentions)
paper_process.narrative: No paper process has started. No MSA, SOW, Order Form, or contracting artifact has been mentioned in any call, task, or Next Step across the full 10 Dec 2025–9 Jul 2026 history — expected, since the deal has not reached commercial negotiation.

economic_buyer.narrative: No confirmed economic buyer exists. Tim Drost (VP Sourcing Operations) is the only executive to appear on a call — on 17 Dec 2025 he said Pep was 'engaging a number of folks' to 'make a decision on a path forward,' but he has not rejoined a call or replied to outreach since (last seen 17 Dec 2025, ~7 months ago, despite five logged re-engagement attempts between 13 Mar and 11 May 2026). No CFO, CPO, or named budget owner appears anywhere.

decision_process.narrative: No formal decision process, approval chain, or gate has ever been named. On 17 Dec 2025 Tim Drost said Pep was still 'evaluating multiple vendors as well as keeping the option to build system,' and Christina Behm's most recent update (26 May 2026, reiterated 9 Jun 2026) only references vague 'internal approvals' with no named step or date.

decision_criteria.narrative: Evaluation criteria are concrete and confirmed. The 13 Feb 2026 scenario session notes state: 'Selection of iContract, iSupplier, and iRisk modules has been confirmed, with preliminary user and admin counts sent to Christina to shape the quote.' This tracks directly to the pain areas named in discovery (exception tracking, contract governance visibility, supplier risk scoring).

value.how_we_win: Position Zycus as the faster, lower-risk, AI-powered path vs a multi-quarter internal build that diverts IT resources from client-facing revenue work. Emphasize out-of-the-box supplier onboarding workflows, contract exception tracking/reporting, and Merlin AI capabilities (contract redlining, clause extraction, risk assessment) that would take 12+ months and significant R&D investment to build in-house. Quantify time-to-value: Zycus live in 10–20 weeks (confirmed 13 Feb scenario call) vs 6–12 month internal build plus ongoing maintenance overhead.
